description: Real-time AI code narration and architectural alignment checker
goals: Auto-detect project context files (README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, etc.)
Provide real-time code analysis via Groq (Llama 3.3 70B)
Suggest better architectural approaches using project context
Learn from developer choices over time
Save and recall project patterns across sessions

constraints.concurrency: Single developer, single project at a time
constraints.latency: <5 seconds per analysis (2 Groq calls max)
constraints.groqRateLimit: 30 RPM free tier — multi-key round-robin reduces pressure

techStack.daemon: Node.js — file watching with chokidar + polling for context files
techStack.backend: Express — Groq API (2-call strategy), Supabase for persistence
techStack.frontend: Next.js React — WebSocket for real-time analysis feed
techStack.ai: Groq (Llama 3.3 70B) — fast, free-tier sufficient for code analysis

keyDecisions.whyNodeDaemon: Event-driven, ideal for file watching with chokidar
keyDecisions.whyGroq: Free tier + fast response + Llama 3.3 70B quality sufficient
keyDecisions.whySupabase: Simple setup, PostgreSQL reliability, RLS security built-in
keyDecisions.why2GroqCalls: Balance between richness (6 analysis dimensions) and API rate limits
keyDecisions.whyAutomatic: Developers should not configure anything — zero setup philosophy
keyDecisions.whyPolling: fs.watch is unreliable on Windows; polling is cross-platform safe

analysisFlow: 1. File change detected by chokidar watcher
3. Daemon sends diff to POST /analyze (with auth token)
4. Backend fetches per-file context from file_knowledge
5. Backend fetches project context from project_context_files
6. upgradeAnalysis.js: Call 1 → suggestion + betterApproach + alignment + decisions
8. Result broadcast to dashboard via WebSocket
9. Dashboard shows EnhancedAnalysis component with 6 sections
fileContextSources: TECH_CHOICES.md — why each technology was chosen
